page: Microsoft Azure D96s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dsv7 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel processor. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per virtual core.

This server configuration does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met using external volumes, though the instance is Premium Storage capable. There are no complimentary public IPv4 addresses bundled with this instance. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and high resource density of 96 vCPUs offer a structured environment for large-scale enterprise workloads requiring predictable compute performance without the overhead of local disk management.

Given the balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and the lack of local storage or GPU accelerators, the D96s_v7 is suited for enterprise-grade general-purpose workloads. These include large-scale web serving, application servers, relational databases utilizing network-attached storage, and batch processing tasks that require significant parallel processing capabilities across 96 dedicated cores.
description: The Microsoft Azure D96s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dsv7 family, utilizing the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs built on an Intel x86_64 architecture, paired with 384.0 GB of system memory, which establishes a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage but is Premium Storage capable, requiring network-attached storage for persistent data. With no integrated GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, the server is designed for resource-dense, compute-heavy general-purpose applications. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for enterprise workloads that require high parallel processing capabilities without local disk overhead. Suitable workloads include large-scale web servers, enterprise application backends, and relational databases.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 96 dedicated Intel vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and Premium Storage capability, optimized for enterprise-scale workloads.
